sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: SV Werder Bremen, sitting at 8th in the league with 47 points, host RB Leipzig, who are positioned slightly higher at 6th with 50 points, at the Weserstadion in Bremen. This matchup promises an exciting clash as both teams are within touching distance in the standings. The referee for the game, Bastian Dankert, averages 4.0 yellow cards per game, which could influence the physicality on the pitch. With RB Leipzig holding a slight edge in points, the game at Weserstadion is set to be a tightly contested battle.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last encounter on January 12, 2025, RB Leipzig dominated SV Werder Bremen with a 4-2 victory at home, with Xavi Simons earning the Player of the Match award with an impressive Sofascore rating of 8.9. Over the last 5 matchups between these two sides, RB Leipzig has secured 2 wins, while 3 games ended in draws, leaving Werder Bremen without a victory. Historically, RB Leipzig has an unbeaten streak of 11 games against Werder Bremen in head-to-head encounters. Additionally, both teams have scored in 5 of their last 6 meetings, indicating a trend of high-scoring and competitive games.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: SV Werder Bremen enters the match with a positive streak of no losses in their last 6 games across all competitions, which could boost their confidence at home. On the other hand, RB Leipzig is struggling with no wins in their last 3 away games and has failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 7 away matches. Leipzig’s away games often feature over 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 6 outings, with both teams scoring in the same number of matches. Werder Bremen’s matches have seen over 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 6 home games, while Leipzig tends to see under 4.5 cards in 6 of their last 7 away fixtures.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - SV Werder Bremen
sections.text: For SV Werder Bremen, Romano Schmid stands out as a creative force in midfield with 62 key passes, ranking him 4th in the league for this metric, alongside 4 goals and 4 assists. Jens Stage has been a prolific scorer with 9 goals, overperforming his expected goals of 6.47 by 2.53, while Marvin Ducksch contributes with 7 goals and 7 assists. Defensively, Anthony Jung impresses with a 70.9% ground duels won percentage, placing him 4th in the league. However, Werder Bremen will miss forward Justin Njinmah, who has 3 goals this season, due to injury.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - RB Leipzig
sections.text: RB Leipzig boasts dangerous attackers like Benjamin Šeško, who leads with 13 goals, overperforming his expected goals of 9.87 by 3.13. Xavi Simons, a standout midfielder, has 9 goals and 7 assists with an outstanding Sofascore rating of 7.56, and was recently in form with 2 goals and 2 assists in the last 5 matches. Loïs Openda, with 9 goals and 5 assists, is another threat, ranking 4th in the league for times fouled per game at 2.4. Unfortunately, Leipzig will be without key defender Willi Orbán, who has 5 goals, and creative full-back David Raum, who has 5 assists.

sections.subtitle: Standout Performers in Previous Matchups
sections.text: In the last 5 encounters against RB Leipzig, Werder Bremen’s goalkeeper Michael Zetterer has been a standout with an average Sofascore rating of 7.33 over 3 starts. His remarkable average of 4.67 saves per game has often kept Werder Bremen competitive in these fixtures. While he hasn’t contributed with goals or assists, his presence between the sticks could be vital for Werder Bremen to contain Leipzig’s potent attack.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Playing Style
sections.text: SV Werder Bremen has scored 50 goals this season, ranking 9th in the league, but their defense has conceded 56 goals, placing them 14th for least goals conceded. They excel in duels, ranking 4th for ground duels won at 51.8% and 9th for aerial duels at 50.5%, while their 162 accurate crosses rank 4th league-wide. RB Leipzig, on the other hand, shows attacking intent with strong second-half performances, scoring 11 goals each in the 16-30 and 46-60 minute periods. However, their away defensive frailties could be exploited by Werder Bremen’s fast breaks, where they rank 4th with 1.28 per game.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Trends
sections.text: SV Werder Bremen has hit the woodwork 14 times this season, ranking 4th among the unluckiest teams in the league, which could be a factor if their finishing improves. They also convert 40.5% of their big chances, ranking 7th, with 79 big chances created (8th in the league). RB Leipzig’s away games often see both teams scoring, happening in 5 of their last 6 matches, and they tend to concede first in 5 of their last 7 away fixtures. Additionally, head-to-head games between these sides have consistently had under 10.5 corners in all of the last 5 encounters.
